title: Default to authenticated encryption (AEAD)
description: Encrypt with an AEAD construction so confidentiality and integrity come together, instead of composing a cipher and a MAC by hand.
body: Use whenever you encrypt data that an attacker could tamper with, which is essentially always. Do not use a bare cipher mode (ECB/CBC/CTR) without authentication, and avoid hand-composing encrypt-then-MAC unless a reviewed standard requires it. Actions: choose a library AEAD (AES-GCM or ChaCha20-Poly1305), pass associated data for context binding, and reject any message whose tag fails verification before using the plaintext. Validation signals include a test that a flipped ciphertext byte causes a verification failure, not silent garbage. Risk boundary: unauthenticated encryption lets attackers tamper undetected (padding-oracle, bit-flipping); treat any non-AEAD encryption on attacker-reachable data as a high-severity defect and escalate.
